without_skill_contaminated.note: DISCARDED — agent read SKILL.md from codebase. See without_skill/ directory for raw data.

failures: Missing skills count in environment collection (3/4 fields, no auto-collection)
No privacy review — jumps from content to submission options without user confirmation
No privacy review — presents content then jumps to submission instructions without user confirmation

analysis.contamination_resolved: Eval 1 re-run with explicit isolation (forbidden from reading aria/skills/aria-report/). Clean baseline confirms the original contamination — delta jumps from 0.0 to +0.5.
analysis.unique_value: Two consistent differentiators across both evals: (1) MANDATORY privacy review before submission — vanilla Claude never does this; (2) Complete auto-collected environment info including skills count — vanilla Claude misses fields or leaves placeholders.
analysis.token_efficiency: with_skill uses comparable tokens (25K avg) to isolated without_skill (24K avg) but achieves 100% pass rate vs 62.5%. The contaminated run used 69K tokens (exploring codebase), confirming structured guidance prevents wasteful exploration.
analysis.content_quality: Vanilla Claude produces excellent content quality (detailed root cause analysis, alternatives considered). The skill's value is NOT content quality but process guarantees: privacy review, complete environment collection, and consistent submission routing.

strengths: Consistent 7-step structured execution across both evals (pass_rate: 1.0)
Mandatory privacy review prevents unreviewed public submissions (0% without_skill compliance)
Complete auto-collected environment info (4/4 fields vs 3/4 or fewer without skill)
Correct routing logic with actual command generation
Token-efficient: structured plan avoids codebase exploration overhead
weaknesses: Only 2 eval cases — limited statistical confidence
Content quality is comparable (vanilla Claude writes excellent issue content)
Initial without_skill baseline was contaminated, requiring re-run
recommendations: Consider adding eval cases for no-token routing scenario and question type
For future AB tests in this repo, use worktree isolation to prevent codebase contamination
